resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def gcdOfStrings(self, str1: str, str2: str) -> str:
        def gcd(a, b):
            while b:
                a, b = b, a % b
            return a
        g = gcd(len(str1), len(str2))
        if str1[:g] * (len(str1) // g) == str1 and str1[:g] * (len(str2) // g) == str2:
            return str1[:g]
        return ""
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(min(len(str1), len(str2))))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses the mathematical concept of Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) to find the largest common divisor of the lengths of the two strings. It then checks if the prefix of the length of the GCD can be repeated to form both strings. If so, it returns the prefix; otherwise, it returns an empty string.
